# REQ-FR-260023: Pre-Search Visibility of Recommended Labels

- Class: FR
- Status: Accepted
- Date: 2026-05-08
- Source decision: Search interaction model update (recommended labels visibility)

## Requirement

The frontend shall keep recommended label suggestions visible near the search bar before any search submission, so users can discover likely starting labels without first issuing a query.

## Rationale

Visible suggestions improve first-use guidance and reduce uncertainty about how to begin a search in a blank-first workspace.

## Scope Notes

- In scope for this requirement: pre-search visibility of recommended labels as search affordances.
- Out of scope for this requirement: the data source for recommendations, ranking logic, and large-scale cap behavior.

## Verification

- Runtime check confirms recommended labels are visible before first search submission.
- End-to-end flow confirms selecting a recommended label can initiate/refine user search workflow.
- Regression checks confirm blank-first workspace behavior remains unchanged before search.

## Traceability

- Related ADRs: ADR-260016
- Related backlog items: BI-260021
